provincial_monitoring_en: Official notification of the alert occurred on 8 May 2026 following rumours of grouped deaths. The epidemiological situation and line lists of suspected and probable cases were updated. Sample collection, analysis, and shipment to INRB were carried out.

rveillance teams were briefed on alerts, investigations, active case search, contact listing, and follow-up. The PoE/PoC team was briefed on mapping of their sites. The surveillance pillar was organised, including identification of focal points per health zone and definition of roles and responsibilities. Surveillance teams were deployed in three epidemic health zones.

Confirmed case mapping was done in two health zones, active case search in four, and EVD alert investigations in eight. At PoE Ofoo (Aru HZ), a traveler from Bukavu via Kigali and Uganda heading to Durba was identified. At PoE Kasenyi (Tchomia HZ), a dead body from Kampala, Uganda, bound for Bunia city was identified.

Supported two health zones with investigation and contact listing around confirmed cases. Set up an alert management system and promoted the civil-protection hotline. Began contact follow-up around one confirmed case. Conducted investigations and contact listing around four confirmed cases in North Kivu.

Terms of Reference for deployment of provincial multidisciplinary teams in one health zone were transmitted to coordination. Terms of Reference for training of actors (ECZS, IT & ITA, community agent leads, data managers) in 5 active health zones were transmitted to coordination. Thermo-flash were provided in another health zone (4 units).

Draft Terms of Reference were developed for deployment of provincial multidisciplinary teams in one health zone. Draft TOR were also developed for training of actors (ECZS, IT & ITA, community agent leads, data managers) in 5 active health zones. Thermo-flash were provided in two health zones for contact follow-up (12 and 11 units respectively).
